title: Rivian Q3 2024 Earnings Preview
text: Rivian will report third-quarter earnings Thursday after market close. Analysts expect revenue of $1.00 billion, marking the company's lowest revenue in six quarters and down from $1.34 billion year-over-year. The company is projected to report a loss of 92 cents per share, improving from a $1.19 loss last year. JPMorgan analyst Ryan Brinkman, who holds an Underweight rating, is particularly focused on demand softness. He noted that The softer trend to production (and possibly deliveries also) we think could endanger the company's still growth stock multiple. Rivian recently lowered annual production guidance to 47,000-49,000 vehicles from 57,000 due to component shortages, yet maintained delivery guidance at 50,000-52,000 units. The company added 3,139 units to inventory in Q3—substantially higher than typical quarterly changes—suggesting potential weak demand. The article notes this will be among the first automotive earnings presentations following Trump's 2024 election victory. Analysts and investors want clarity on how the new administration might affect the EV sector and Rivian's strategy. Rivian shares were trading at $10.20, down 53% year-to-date.
